fr.elements: Main Building; Vehicle Parking Area; Internal Drive Aisle; Landscaped Area; Detached Structure
fr.considerations: Single Access Point; Open Terrain; Multiple Structures
fr.recommendations: Enhancing emergency responder access to the property could be achieved by ensuring that the single access point remains clearly marked and unobstructed at all times. This may facilitate quick entry and exit for emergency vehicles. Maintaining open terrain around the main building and the detached structure could support effective movement and staging areas for responders. Clearly delineating the internal drive aisle can aid in navigation and reduce response times. Efficient organization of the vehicle parking area to prevent congestion may further enhance maneuverability for emergency equipment. Strategic landscaping that does not impede visibility or access routes could improve overall operational safety and efficiency. Clearly marked routes to each structure would also assist in streamlined navigation during emergency operations.

wildfire.conditions: Structure Cluster; Vegetated Perimeter; Open Terrain; Scattered Shrubs; Limited Defensible Space; Vegetation Adjacent to Structure; Vegetation Adjacent to Access Route; Open Fuel Break; Single Access Route; Scattered Trees
wildfire.recommendations: Enhancing defensible space around the property could be achieved by managing the vegetation that is adjacent to structures and the access route. Increasing separation between vegetation and the structure, as well as along the property's perimeter, could reduce fuel continuity, thereby improving wildfire resilience. Consider maintaining and expanding the open fuel break and managing the scattered shrubs and trees to further support this effort. Attention to the vegetation near the single access route would support firefighter access and suppression efforts. Maintaining open terrain can be beneficial in preserving and expanding fuel breaks, contributing to long-term fire mitigation strategies and improving overall property preparedness against wildfire exposure.
